metadata.state: Ministry Of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

raw_data.details: The National Talent Scholarship (NTS) is implemented by the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) under the scheme "Strengthening and Development of Higher Agricultural Education in India". The scholarship is awarded to students pursuing a Bachelor's degree in Agriculture and allied disciplines who have secured admission through the All India Entrance Examination (AIEE) and are studying in an ICAR-funded Agricultural University located outside their State of domicile. The scholarship aims to support meritorious students by providing financial assistance during their undergraduate studies. The scholarship is initially awarded for one year from the date of admission and is renewable for the remaining duration of the degree programme, subject to satisfactory academic performance and conduct as prescribed by the concerned university.
raw_data.benefits: ₹3,000 per month as scholarship assistance during the Bachelor's degree programme. Note: No contingency grant is provided under the scheme.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be pursuing a Bachelor's degree programme in Agriculture or allied disciplines. The applicant should have secured admission through the All India Entrance Examination (AIEE) conducted for ICAR admissions. The applicant should be enrolled in an Agricultural University located outside his/her State of domicile. The applicant should be studying in an Agricultural University funded under the ICAR scheme "Strengthening and Development of Higher Agricultural Education in India". Conditions for Continuation of ScholarshipThe student must maintain merit and good conduct as certified by the Head of the College/University/Institution. The student must attend classes regularly and not remain absent without prior permission. The student must not participate in strikes, agitations, or activities that disrupt academic studies. The student must pursue studies on a full-time basis and not undertake any part-time or full-time employment. The student must not receive any other scholarship or financial assistance during the course of study. The student must not leave the course within one month of admission. The student must not change the discipline/subject initially allotted for which the scholarship was awarded. The student must not change the University during the tenure of the scholarship. The student must secure a minimum OGPA/CGPA of 7. 00 out of 10. 00 at the end of the first year (6. 50 out of 10. 00 for SC/ST students). The student must clear all papers in every semester and appear in regular examinations.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineNew Registration:
Step 1: Visit the [New Registration](https://scholarships.gov.in/otrapplication/#/) page of the National Scholarships Portal.
Step 2: Guidelines for Registration will appear. Scroll to the bottom. Read the undertaking carefully and Accept the Terms. Click “Next”.
Step 3: In the next window, enter your mobile number and OTP. Then click “Verify”.
Step 4: After successful verification, continue with e-KYC process (as applicable).
Step 5: Once completed, your reference number will be generated and the same will be sent to your registered phone number. Process for OTR ID Number Generation:
Step 1: Download and install [AadhaarFaceRD](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.gov.uidai.facerd) services in the android device.
Step 2: Download and install the [NSP OTR](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.gov.scholarships.nspotr&pli=1) App from the Google Play Store.
Step 3: After opening the mobile app Select the option “eKYC with FaceAuth” highlighted in red.
Step 4: Enter the allotted reference number and click on “Send OTP” to receive OTP on your mobile no.
Step 5: Enter the received OTP and captcha in the screen and click Next.
Step 6: Click on “Proceed for Face Authentication” button highlighted in red.
Step 7: UIDAI AadhaarFaceRD services will be invoked for face authentication. Ensure AadhaarFaceRD services are preinstalled on your device.
Step 8: On Successful completion of face-authentication OTR no. will be generated and will also be sent on your registered mobile no. Fresh Application:
Step 1: Visit the applicant [Login](https://scholarships.gov.in/otrapplication/#/login-page)page of the National Scholarships Portal Enter your One Time Registration (OTR) number and password.
Step 2: Type the Captcha and click “Login”.
Step 3: On the next screen, provide the OTP received on your registered mobile number. You will be directed to the Password Reset screen. Create a new password and confirm.
Step 4: Click “Submit”. You will be directed to the “Applicant’s Dashboard”.
Step 5: On the left pane, click “Application Form”. The fields marked as * are compulsory. Fill in the details and upload the documents.
Step 6: You can either click on “Save as Draft” to complete the application later. Else, click “Final Submit” to submit the application. Track Payment Status:The student can track their payment status from the template “Know Your Payment” in the [Public Financial Management System (PFMS) Portal](https://pfms.nic.in/static/NewLayoutCommonContent.aspx? RequestPagename=static/TrackNSPpayments.aspx) either by indicating their Aadhaar Number, Bank Account number or NSP Application ID. NOTE 1: The National Scholarships Portal will provide the timelines for opening & closing of the portal. NOTE 2: Online applications will be verified at two levels i.e. by the Institute, where the student is studying and thereafter by the concerned State Higher Education Department or any other Agency/ Official nominated by the State as State Nodal Agency (both for fresh and renewal applicants). NOTE 3: Students will not be permanently debarred for scholarships due to delay in renewal or verification. However, students will have to submit renewal applications within a cut-off date. Renewal for that year will not be allowed if an applicant misses this deadline.
raw_data.documents_required: Recent passport-size photograph. Signature. Thumb impression. Admission proof through AIEE. Aadhaar number. Aadhaar-linked bank account details. Any other documents prescribed by ICAR or the concerned University.

The scholarship aims to support meritorious students by providing financial assistance during their undergraduate studies. The applicant should be enrolled in an Agricultural University located outside his/her State of domicile. The applicant should be studying in an Agricultural University funded under the ICAR scheme "Strengthening and Development of Higher Agricultural Education in India". The student must attend classes regularly and not remain absent without prior permission. The student must not participate in strikes, agitations, or activities that disrupt academic studies. The student must not receive any other scholarship or financial assistance during the course of study. The student must not leave the course within one month of admission. Step 2: Download and install the [NSP OTR](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=in.gov.scholarships.nspotr&pli=1) App from the Google Play Store. Step 3: After opening the mobile app Select the option “eKYC with FaceAuth” highlighted in red. Step 4: Enter the allotted reference number and click on “Send OTP” to receive OTP on your mobile no. Step 5: Enter the received OTP and captcha in the screen and click Next. Step 6: Click on “Proceed for Face Authentication” button highlighted in red. NOTE 1: The National Scholarships Portal will provide the timelines for opening & closing of the portal. NOTE 2: Online applications will be verified at two levels i.e. by the Institute, where the student is studying and thereafter by the concerned State Higher Education Department or any other Agency/ Official nominated by the State as State Nodal Agency (both for fresh and renewal applicants). NOTE 3: Students will not be permanently debarred for scholarships due to delay in renewal or verification. However, students will have to submit renewal applications within a cut-off date. Renewal for that year will not be allowed if an applicant misses this deadline. Documents RequiredRecent passport-size photograph. Signature. Thumb impression. Admission proof through AIEE. Aadhaar number. Aadhaar-linked bank account details. Any other documents prescribed by ICAR or the concerned University. Frequently Asked QuestionsWho can apply for the National Talent Scholarship? Students admitted through the All India Entrance Examination (AIEE) to an eligible Agricultural University located outside their State of domicile can apply. Is admission through AIEE necessary to avail the scholarship? Yes. Only students admitted through the All India Entrance Examination (AIEE) are eligible for the scholarship. Can students studying in their home State receive the scholarship? No. The scholarship is available only to students studying in an eligible Agricultural University outside their State of domicile. How long can a student receive the scholarship? The scholarship is awarded initially for one year and can be renewed for the remaining duration of the course based on academic performance and conduct. What academic requirements must be met for renewal of the scholarship? Students must maintain the prescribed OGPA/CGPA, clear all semester examinations, and maintain satisfactory conduct. Can a student receive another scholarship while availing NTS? No. Students receiving the National Talent Scholarship cannot avail any other scholarship or financial assistance during the course. What happens if a student changes the allotted discipline or University? The scholarship will be discontinued if the student changes the discipline or transfers to another University. How is the scholarship amount disbursed to students? The scholarship is released through the concerned University and credited to the student's Aadhaar-linked bank account through DBT. Under what circumstances can the scholarship be discontinued? The scholarship may be discontinued due to poor academic performance, failure in examinations, indiscipline, change of discipline, or acceptance of another scholarship. What happens if a student withdraws from the course before completion? The scholarship will be stopped, and the student may be required to refund the scholarship amount received as per ICAR guidelines. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://scholarships.gov.in/public/schemeGuidelines/GuidelinesforICAR_NTS_Scholarship_3103_3104.pdf)
[FAQs](https://scholarships.gov.in/public/FAQ/FAQsforICAR_NTS-UG_3103.pdf)OkWas this helpful?

faqs.question: Who can apply for the National Talent Scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students admitted through the All India Entrance Examination (AIEE) to an eligible Agricultural University located outside their State of domicile can apply.

faqs.question: Is admission through AIEE necessary to avail the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes. Only students admitted through the All India Entrance Examination (AIEE) are eligible for the scholarship.

faqs.question: Can students studying in their home State receive the scholarship?
faqs.answer: No. The scholarship is available only to students studying in an eligible Agricultural University outside their State of domicile.

faqs.question: How long can a student receive the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is awarded initially for one year and can be renewed for the remaining duration of the course based on academic performance and conduct.

faqs.question: What academic requirements must be met for renewal of the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students must maintain the prescribed OGPA/CGPA, clear all semester examinations, and maintain satisfactory conduct.

faqs.question: Can a student receive another scholarship while availing NTS?
faqs.answer: No. Students receiving the National Talent Scholarship cannot avail any other scholarship or financial assistance during the course.

faqs.question: What happens if a student changes the allotted discipline or University?
faqs.answer: The scholarship will be discontinued if the student changes the discipline or transfers to another University.

faqs.question: How is the scholarship amount disbursed to students?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is released through the concerned University and credited to the student's Aadhaar-linked bank account through DBT.

faqs.question: Under what circumstances can the scholarship be discontinued?
faqs.answer: The scholarship may be discontinued due to poor academic performance, failure in examinations, indiscipline, change of discipline, or acceptance of another scholarship.

faqs.question: What happens if a student withdraws from the course before completion?
faqs.answer: The scholarship will be stopped, and the student may be required to refund the scholarship amount received as per ICAR guidelines.
